behaviors.description: Data collection includes results from all three sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, deployment log)
behaviors.justification: This is a data composition test. The failure (omitting one data source) is caught by mocking all three sources and verifying the collected result contains data from all of them. The failure boundary is local logic (function composition), not external behavior. No real infrastructure required.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three data sources with fixed return values. Call the data collection function. Assert the result contains expected data from all three sources (metrics, issues, deployments).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test operates entirely in-process with mocks; no real APIs or I/O required.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, report is produced with 'data unavailable' note for that section instead of aborting
behaviors.justification: This tests error handling in the data collection step. By mocking one source to raise an exception, we verify the code catches it and produces valid partial output. The failure mode (aborting vs. degrading gracefully) is entirely testable with mocks simulating failures. No real infrastructure required.
behaviors.test_description: Mock three data sources; configure one to raise an exception (simulating unavailability). Call data collection. Verify result contains valid data from the two working sources and includes a 'data unavailable' note for the third.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Error handling logic is fully testable with exception-raising mocks.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data
behaviors.justification: This behavior directly depends on real LLM output quality. Mocking the LLM to always return a predetermined summary hides the real failure: the real model might omit key values or misrepresent them. Only a real LLM call can answer 'does this particular model actually capture all the key values when given this data?' The failure boundary is LLM output quality, which is non-deterministic and model-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Create structured test data with specific labeled key values (e.g., metric values, issue counts, deployment times). Call the real LLM with a prompt that includes this data. Verify the generated summary contains all key values through parsing or structured verification.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: LLM output quality is inherently non-deterministic; mocking would hide the real failure mode.

behaviors.description: A complete report from data collection through email delivery reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: Email delivery is the critical failure boundary. Mocking the email service hides real failures: SMTP timeouts, authentication failures, recipient filtering, malformed headers, oversized payloads, or service-side rate limiting. Real email delivery requires a real email service (or test SMTP server). Additionally, real data sources (or staging servers) are needed to ensure the full pipeline works end-to-end. The LLM is also involved (Agentic component), but the primary failure boundary for 'reaches the inbox' is System-level email delivery infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Using a test email server (real or containerized SMTP), execute the complete pipeline: collect data from real/staging sources, generate summary via real LLM, send via real email service. Verify the email is successfully delivered to a test inbox and content is intact and not mangled.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Email delivery and data sources cannot be adequately mocked without hiding real failure modes like SMTP errors, network issues, or payload problems.

behaviors.description: When email delivery service is unreachable, generated report is saved to local file and delivery is retried on next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: This tests fallback and retry logic in response to email failure. The failure modes (report not saved to file, or not retried on next execution) are caught by mocking the email service to fail, verifying file save, then simulating the next scheduled run and verifying the retry. The 'next scheduled run' is simulated by calling the retry logic function—no real scheduler required. All logic is testable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Mock email service to fail (raise exception). Call report generation. Verify report file is created on disk. Mock email service to succeed. Call retry logic (simulating next scheduled execution). Verify report is read from file, email is sent successfully, and the file is deleted/archived.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Fallback logic and file state management can be tested entirely with mocks and function calls.
